item.title: About
item.description:
Jian Zhou (周健)

You can call me Jack.

I am an independent researcher based in Shanghai, China. My research lies at the intersection of number theory, mathematical physics, and condensed matter theory.

Professional Life

By day, I work as Director of Product and Game Producer at XD.INC (HKEX: 02400), a Hong Kong-listed gaming company. I lead cross-platform game development teams working with Unreal Engine 5.

Berlin Years (2017–2020)

Research Motivation

The fine-structure constant α has long fascinated me — this mysterious dimensionless number that Feynman himself called “one of the greatest damn mysteries of physics.” I have been driven by a simple question: can this constant admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression?

item.title: Publications
item.description:
The Fine-Structure Constant α

  • Spectral Invariants of the Bost-Connes System over Q(sqrt5) and an Arithmetic Framework for the Three-Term Expansion of the Inverse Fine-Structure Constant

    2026-04-04 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31891384 | 👁️ 46 | 📥 5

    2026-03-24 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31840012 | 👁️ 57 | 📥 33
  • α = cos²(π/5)/90: The Geometric Anatomy of the Fine-Structure Constant
    2026-03-23 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31839535 | 👁️ 38 | 📥 12

    2026-03-23 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31839511 | 👁️ 40 | 📥 4
  • A Zeta-Function Reformulation of Wyler's Geometric Formula for the Fine-Structure Constant
    2026-03-17 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31771282 | 👁️ 56 | 📥 151

Spherical Membrane Model of Particles

  • Gap Symmetry Is Orbital: Electron Membrane Resonance Picture and Validation in Multiorbital Superconductors
    2026-04-20 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.32049069 | 👁️ 17 | 📥 2
  • Photons as Expanding Spherical Membranes: A Geometric Reinterpretation of Light from Newton's Prism to Einstein's Eclipse
    2026-03-31 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31899475 | 👁️ 44 | 📥 1
  • Electrons as Spherical Membranes: A Unified Geometric Framework for Bound and Free States
    2026-03-31 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31898347 | 👁️ 61 | 📥 13

Room-Temperature Superconductivity

  • Two-Channel Allen-Dynes Framework for Superconducting Critical Temperatures: Blind Predictions Across Five Orders of Magnitude and a Quantum-Metric No-Go Result
    2026-03-30 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31889344 | 👁️ 19 | 📥 4

    2026-03-23 | DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.31833382 | 👁️ 30 | 📥 25
  • Band-basis decomposition of superfluid weight in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene: Quantifying geometric and conventional contributions

item.title: Research
item.description:
Research Directions

1. The Fine-Structure Constant α

Central Question: Can the fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 admit an elegant and exact mathematical expression rooted in number theory?

Key Result (2026): The three-term formula

α⁻¹ = 360/φ² − 2/φ³ + 1/(3φ)⁵ = 137.035999165…

agrees with CODATA 2022 to 0.58σ — within experimental uncertainty. All three coefficients are intrinsic arithmetic invariants of the real quadratic field K = ℚ(√5).

The arithmetic framework (Bost-Connes spectral theory):
